title: Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere
tagline: Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Shankareshvara temple (also spelt "Shankareshwara" or "Sankaresvara"), dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva is located in Turuvekere, a small town in the Tumkur district, Karnataka
overview.description: The Shankareshvara temple (also spelt "Shankareshwara" or "Sankaresvara"), dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva is located in Turuvekere, a small town in the Tumkur district, Karnataka state, India. Turuvekere, founded as an Agraharam town (a place of learning) in the 13th century is located about 77 miles from the state capital Bangalore. The temple was built around 1260 A.D. during the rule of the Hoysala Empire King Narasimha III. This temple is a protected monument under the Karnataka state division of the Archaeological Survey of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere?
faq.a: The best time to visit Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (104 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (95 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere?
faq.a: Stays in Moole Shankareshvara Temple, Turuvekere start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
